none: A seated woman smiles at an interviewer as two men sit on either side of her.

body.creator: Tallulah Stallvik
body.value: Rubio Gurrerro is based on Barbara Hutton’s fifth husband, Dominican diplomat and playboy Porfirio Rubirosa, who was “raised and educated in Paris, attending the finest schools and early in life circulating among the highest ranks of society” (Haymann, Poor Little Rich Girl, 286). Porfirio was as well known for his taste for the extravagant as for his “prodigal dimensions,” which, allegedly, inspired the use of his surname for requesting freshly ground pepper “in the finest European restaurants” (Haymann, Poor Little Rich Girl, 289). Their marriage lasted 53 days.

body.creator: Tallulah Stallvik
body.value: This press conference took place in real life at the Pierre hotel in New York, not London. Barbara Hutton’s biographer describes the scene as follows: “At three o’clock on December 30, an hour before the wedding, a band of reporters trudged into Barbara’s living room at the Pierre. Barbara sat on a light-green settee, next to Lance. She wore an upswept hairdo and diamonds that sparkled in a brooch on the scoop neckline of a black taffeta dress by Balenciaga [one of the primary inspirations for Reynolds’s character!], complemented by diamond-and-pearl earrings and a pair of diamond bracelets. She had agreed to the press conference, but looked as though she now regretted it” (Haymann, Poor Little Rich Girl, 295). The film quotes nearly verbatim this press conference, as described by Haymann. For instance, when asked about who will serve as attendant at the wedding, Hutton responded, “‘My son.... My son is wonderful. He’s all in favor of the marriage.’ Lance smiled but looked as uncomfortable as his mother” (Haymann, Poor Little Rich Girl, 295).
